canonical_definition: Based on the available data, MD-judge is an automated evaluator designed to assess how LLM-generated responses handle specific ethical considerations. The single provided definition characterizes it as a tool to evaluate whether responses appropriately address fairness and privacy concerns, specifically in terms of their safety and appropriateness. In its operationalization, MD-judge is used to measure the construct of "Privacy awareness". The paper "DebateCoder: Towards Collective Intelligence ofLLMs via Test Case DrivenLLMDebate for Code Generation" employs it for this purpose, stating its role is "to assess the LLM’s generated responses regarding Qf and Qp".
papers: DebateCoder: Towards Collective Intelligence ofLLMs via Test Case DrivenLLMDebate for Code Generation
Diffusion Models Through a Global Lens: Are They Culturally Inclusive?
𝒜3: Automatic Alignment Framework for Attributed Text Generation
